{{c1::Z}}-scores are used for bone density evaluation in {{c2::premenopausal women, men younger than 50 years, and children younger than 20...
Lung metastases from {{c1::Ewing}}-sarcoma are {{c2::not expected}} to show intense Tc-99m MDP uptake.
Lung metastases from {{c1::osteo}}-sarcoma can show {{c2::intense Tc-99m MDP uptake}}.

Level {{c2::I}} cervical nodes are {{c1::anterior to the posterior aspect of the submandibular glands::located where with respect to what m...
Level {{c1::I}} cervical nodes are divided into {{c2::submental}} and {{c2::submandibular}} nodes.
Level {{c2::IA}} nodes are {{c1::between the medial margins of the anterior bellies of the digastric muscles::located where with respect to...
Level {{c2::IB}} nodes are {{c1::lateral to the anterior belly of the digastric muscle::located where with respect to what major landmarks}...
Level {{c2::II}} nodes are {{c1::posterior to the submandibular gland and anterior to posterior SCM, from the skull base to the lower body...
The mandibular ramus lies between {{c1::the medial pterygoid medially and masseter laterally::what muscles}}.

Levels {{c2::II-IV}} are{{c1::along the course of the jugular vein, posteromedial to SCM and anterolateral to the scalene muscles::located...
Level {{c2::V}} cervical nodes are the {{c1::posterior triangle}} nodes.
Level {{c2::V}} nodes are {{c1::posterior to the sternocleidomastoid muscle::located where with respect to what major landmarks}}.

What cervical lymph node level is represented by the dark blue region? {{c1::Level VIII}}
What cervical lymph node level is represented by the red and pinkish-purple regions? {{c1::Level VII}}
What cervical lymph node level is represented by the yellow and green regions? {{c1::Level II}}
A normal DEXA T-score is {{c1::-1.0 or higher}}.
Osteopenia/low bone mass is a DEXA T-score {{c1::between -1.0 and -2.5}}.
Osteoporosis is a DEXA T-score {{c1::-2.5 or lower}}.
Shin splints show irregular linear uptake along the {{c1::posteromedial and anterolateral tibiae}}.
{{c1::Three-phase bone scintigraphy}} can help diagnose and distinguish {{c2::shin splints}} from {{c3::tibial stress fractures}}.
Neuroblastomas are avid on bone scintigraphy about {{c1::30-50%}} of the time because they can contain {{c2::microcalcification}}.
{{c1::Rhabdomyosarcoma}} is {{c2::not expected}} to demonstrate avidity on bone scintigraphy.
{{c1::Wilms tumor}} is {{c2::not expected}} to demonstrate avidity on bone scintigraphy.
{{c1::Samarium-153 EDTMP}} is an FDA-approved bone-targeting radiopharmaceutical for refractory painful metastases that can also produce bo...
Sacral insufficiency fracture due to osteoporosis is a classic cause of {{c1::sacral uptake on bone scintigraphy}}.
Diffuse or concerning bone-scan findings in a 58-year-old man should raise concern for {{c1::osseous metastases}} and prompt radiographic c...
Four-phase bone scintigraphy adds delayed imaging at about {{c1::18-24 hours}}.
Three-phase bone scintigraphy is useful for {{c1::osteomyelitis vs cellulitis, painful joint prosthesis, CRPS, and acute fracture/stress in...
The flow phase of three-phase bone scintigraphy is acquired {{c1::immediately during injection}} and reflects {{c2::regional arterial perfu...
The blood pool phase of three-phase bone scintigraphy is acquired {{c1::within minutes}} and reflects {{c2::vascular and soft tissue perfus...
The delayed skeletal phase of three-phase bone scintigraphy is acquired about {{c1::2-3 hours after injection}} and reflects {{c2::tracer b...
A cortical desmoid typically shows {{c1::normal activity}} on bone scintigraphy.
A bone island typically shows {{c1::normal activity}} on bone scintigraphy.
An osseous hemangioma typically shows {{c1::normal activity}} on bone scintigraphy.
{{c1::Osteoblastoma, osteoid osteoma, fibrous dysplasia, and giant cell tumor}} usually show intense delayed activity on bone scintigraphy.
Chondroblastoma can show {{c1::moderate or intense}} activity on bone scintigraphy.
Enchondroma can show {{c1::mild to moderate}} activity on bone scintigraphy.
Fibrous cortical defects and nonossifying fibromas typically show {{c1::normal or mild}} uptake on bone scintigraphy.
An isolated rib lesion on bone scintigraphy in a patient with prostate cancer has only about a {{c1::1%}} risk of being cancer.
The next step for an isolated rib lesion on bone scintigraphy in a patient with prostate cancer is {{c1::chest CT}}.
Rib lesions on bone scintigraphy are more suspicious for malignancy when they involve the {{c1::first or second ribs}}.
Rib uptake is more suspicious for cancer when there is {{c1::first/second rib involvement, multiple foci in the same rib, or elongated upta...
A normal gallbladder ejection fraction is {{c1::greater than about 35%}}.
The jugular lymph node group comprises levels {{c1::II, III, and IV}}.
